_provenance: MACHINE-CAPTURED 2026-07-18 (Task 6, docs/loom/plans/2026-07-18-52-53-week-filer-support.md). Recipe: `uv run --with 'requests==2.33.1' --with 'edgartools==5.42.0' investing-toolkit/skills/data-markets/scripts/pack.py --ticker COST --pack kpi-quarterly` against the LIVE SEC EDGAR data.sec.gov API (user-agent per sec_edgar_client.py's own header convention), yielding 393 real dimensional-revenue facts across COST's captured filing history; this fixture is a hand-selected SUBSET (never hand-typed values — every field below is a verbatim copy from the captured pack.py output) of the `us-gaap:RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax|ProductOrService=MembershipMember` signature's FY2024 facts: Q1 (0000909832-23-000065), Q2 3mo + H1 (0000909832-24-000017), Q3 3mo + 36wk-YTD (0000909832-24-000029), FY (0000909832-24-000049) — all four accessions' dei fiscal_year_end agree (--09-01), so Q4 derives cleanly (FY total minus 36wk-YTD = 16-week Q4, the 52-week-FY member of the week-Q4 band). The Q3 period_end (2024-05-12) sits EXACTLY 16 weeks before the filing's own FYE (2024-09-01) — the real-data instance of Gate P's COST-shaped acceptance case (Task 2). The H1 fact (duration_months=5, real, not synthesized) exercises the week-lane's H1/24wk-YTD class on genuine XBRL data. Scoped to ONE fiscal year deliberately: the only OTHER captured fiscal year with a matching-calendar Q4 basis (FY2025, also 52 weeks) would give an EQUAL-week comparator (no week_normalized_yoy to assert), and the one available DIFFERENT-week real pair (FY2023 vs FY2024) hits a genuine `q4_basis_mismatch` refusal — FY2023's Q3-YTD comparative was only captured embedded inside the FY2024 Q3 10-Q (accession 0000909832-24-000029), whose OWN declared fiscal_year_end (--09-01, FY2024's) differs from the FY2023 10-K's own (--09-03) — a real per-filing-calendar divergence the fail-closed guard correctly refuses, not a fixture defect. The different-week week_normalized_yoy positive case is covered instead by the sibling fixture xbrl_quarterly_cost_week_lane_yoy_synth.json. Regenerate via the same pack.py recipe against a fresh fetch — never hand-edit values.
